Reason_for_Visit.summary: 56-year-old female with newly diagnosed metastatic ER+ breast cancer, previously treated with mastectomy in 2013, presenting for initial consult due to widespread metastases involving lungs, peritoneum, liver, and ovary, with local recurrence near the right axilla and implant.

Cancer_Diagnosis.Stage_of_Cancer: Originally Stage IIA, now metastatic (Stage IV)
Cancer_Diagnosis.Metastasis: Yes (to lungs, peritoneum, liver, and ovaries)
Cancer_Diagnosis.Distant Metastasis: Yes (to lungs, peritoneum, liver, and ovaries)

Clinical_Findings.findings: Widespread metastases consistent with metastatic breast carcinoma. Right axillary adenopathy with a 1.8 cm spiculated mass along the superolateral margin of the right breast implant, suspicious for regional recurrence. Multiple small pulmonary nodules, right greater than left, likely metastatic. Large anterior right and left lobe hepatic masses likely metastatic. Peritoneal carcinomatosis with omental nodularity and small possibly malignant effusion. Mildly enlarged and hyperdense ovaries, which could reflect breast metastases to ovaries. Appendix distended with fluid without associated inflammatory wall thickening, suggestive of a mucocele. No CT evidence of osseous metastasis. Palpable hepatomegaly and omental masses in the upper and mid abdomen, non-tender. Right mastectomy site with a 3 cm mass in the axilla.

Response_Assessment.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.

Physical exam: ECOG Performance Status 0 - Asymptomatic.

Clinical_Findings.findings: MRI brain on 08/15/18 showed multiple very small new CNS lesions. CT CAP on 09/05/18 was stable. Recent MRI shows new lesions. MRI brain on 09/05/18 showed no pleural effusion, no mediastinal lymphadenopathy, and stable appearance of diffuse osseous metastatic lesions. CT A/P on 09/05/18 showed interval resolution of right pleural effusion and stable appearance of metastatic disease, including enlarged right cardiophrenic lymph nodes, 9 mm celiac axis node, and diffuse osseous lesions. Physical exam: ECOG 1, BP 112/68, Pulse 66, Temp 36.6 C, Resp 18, Ht 167.3 cm, Wt 67.8 kg, SpO2 98%, BMI 24.24 kg/m. General appearance: Alert, cooperative, no distress, appears stated age.
